title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
summary: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering work from writing individual lines of code to designing agentic systems that require traditional engineering principles like systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns. She contends that engineers will find fulfillment and retain their value by applying existing engineering discipline to higher-level system-building rather than losing it to automation.
writeup: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents move engineering work away from writing individual lines of code and toward designing agentic systems. The talk holds that traditional engineering principles still apply: systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns carry over to building multi-agent systems. The central claim is that engineers retain their value and find fulfillment by applying existing engineering discipline to higher-level system design rather than ceding it to automation.
